title: Fix Pulse — not pulling live data
description: Fix Pulse / not pulling data and info atm. Pulse section should show live system health and usage data.

title: Simplify job card edge animation — thin coloured border + slow REQ pulse only
description: Remove the animation. Need a simple thin coloured edge. REQ number is cycling slowly that is all. Don't touch the task part of the animation. Only edge of the box and the REQ # at the top.

title: Scheduled jobs — create without starting, auto-start on due date
description: I added a few jobs in the future at a future date. The job started straight as I clicked create job — this should create the job but not start it. On the job it should say something like scheduled to start: date and actually start then.

title: Reduce model context weight — archive done jobs on demand
description: Make the jobs a mechanic of current and done. The done list should not add massive trace for token of context when done. The model should only see and know what is worked on now and have access to archives just on demand. No extra weight on the model that grows as we do more jobs.

title: Force dark mode as default — light mode on iPhone
description: Somehow, on my iPhone, Mission Control is light not dark. Force dark mode as default.

title: Add Edit button to unstarted job modals
description: When jobs are not started, we need to be able to edit the job if you have not started it. Add an edit button at the top when you open a job.

title: Build Logs panel — date, REQ#, summary, click to expand
description: Replace Comms Log with a Logs panel under the Pulse menu. Each log entry shows: date, REQ number, last sentence about that request. Click to expand and see the full back-and-forth for that request. Click a log entry to open the job modal.

title: interface change.
details: let's remove the "add to to do" section from the menu panel. Big buttons are "overview", "Production", then agents and then dev. Overview and Production are single button selector. Now once in production, there's a simple + sign at the top right for user to now enter details of a new task. This will clean the menu layout.

Now once in production, there's a simple + sign at the top right for user to now enter details of a new task. This will clean the menu layout.

title: Mission Control UI polish pass
description:
Multiple polish items from Sam + Gemma QC:
1. Subtask click affordance — add cursor:pointer, consider proper checkboxes
2. Dashboard spacing — more breathing room between sections
3. Priority pill vs phase badge distinction
4. Snapshot images higher on Done cards
5. Align Pulse agents with Overview agents (team members not raw sessions)
6. Move Usage section in Pulse above Job Pipeline (after System Health)
7. Replace Claude usage label with Ollama, show model breakdown
8. Use gateway uptime from OpenClaw instead of Python server uptime
9. Mission Control title: bold, fat, one word per line, top-left aligned
10. Theme toggle: just moon/sun icon, no button border, minimal

title: Production tab + approval flow + created-by tracking
description:
Structural changes to Mission Control:
1. Add createdBy field to all jobs (Sam, Alfred, Claude, Gemma)
2. Approval flow: jobs needing Sams sign-off show Awaiting Approval state with accept/deny
3. New Production tab in nav — dedicated working view with To Do + Done columns, priorities, due dates, assignees
4. Overview becomes dashboard-only (agents, stats, health)
5. Remove snapshot buttons/features for now — consolidate layout first
6. Anyone on the team can create jobs, not just Sam

title: Mission Control UX Pass 3
description:
Refinements from Sam testing session:
1. Subtask done meta: show ✓ by Claude (clickable → message) + time taken (startedAt → completedAt duration), not timestamp
2. No ⏱ clock on in-progress subtasks, just pulse + active label
3. Pending subtasks: ⋯ menu on right with Prioritise/Cancel. Cancelled stays visible, ⋯ again to Resume
4. Remove ⬆ arrow from cards. Keep NEXT UP badge only
5. Priority labels clickable but broken — make them actually cycle (low→normal→high→critical)
6. Subtask numbering: REQ-013-1, REQ-013-2 etc in list and history
7. Kimi tokens still showing 0 — need OpenClaw session data, not just codexbar
8. Done jobs: task count should show X tasks completed (not 15/15)
9. Compaction count not showing in Pulse — wire up from session data

title: Overview redesign — Gemma
description:
Overview section needs a redesign. Current state: just agent cards + activity feed. Removed: Tasks Today/Completed/Error Rate stats, System Health section (Uptime, Current Model, Awaiting Approval).

Gemma should propose a new layout for the Overview that makes it feel alive and useful. What should the landing view of Mission Control look like?

Constraints:
- Vanilla JS/CSS only, no frameworks
- Dark/light theme support
- Must work on mobile
- Keep agent cards as the hero element
- Activity feed stays
- System health / uptime lives in Pulse, not Overview

title: UI Tweak
description: in overview, the to do box have a start, pause, top buttons. I want these to be moved down left to the box. reserve a little footer for it and the "click to open" that sits on the right side also can benefit the little footer. I want to have only one button on the left. a circle with a pause sign. You can click to pause the job. The pause becomes then a play button if users want to start again. whilst paused, a x in a circle appears at the top right to delete the job.

title: Build LLM Wiki Presentation Website
description:
Create a visually stunning dark-themed single-page website presenting Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern. Source: https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f

Three layers: Raw Sources, Wiki, Schema. Three operations: Ingest, Query, Lint. Use cases, tooling, Memex lineage. Premium product-page feel with scroll animations, interactive diagrams, 3D isometric layer view. Single HTML file, pure CSS/JS, no frameworks.
details:
Create a visually stunning dark-themed single-page website presenting Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern. Source: https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f

Three layers: Raw Sources, Wiki, Schema. Three operations: Ingest, Query, Lint. Use cases, tooling, Memex lineage. Premium product-page feel with scroll animations, interactive diagrams, 3D isometric layer view. Single HTML file, pure CSS/JS, no frameworks.

subtasks.title: Set up project folder and read Karpathy gist
Build HTML/CSS/JS — dark theme, hero, scroll animations
Interactive 3D layer diagram + operations flow
Use cases, tooling, Memex sections
QA and visual verification

title: Host Mission Control remotely — Railway + VPN access
description:
Research and implement hosting Mission Control so Sam can access it remotely. Two questions to answer:
1. How to deploy the Mission Control server on Railway (or similar)
2. How to access it remotely via home VPN (Tailscale, WireGuard, etc.)

Current setup: Python dev server on localhost:8787, data in JSON files. Need to figure out deployment strategy that keeps it accessible from outside the house.
details:
Research and implement hosting Mission Control so Sam can access it remotely. Two questions to answer:
1. How to deploy the Mission Control server on Railway (or similar)
2. How to access it remotely via home VPN (Tailscale, WireGuard, etc.)

Current setup: Python dev server on localhost:8787, data in JSON files. Need to figure out deployment strategy that keeps it accessible from outside the house.

subtasks.title: Research Railway deployment for Python/JS static server + JSON data
Research VPN options for remote access to self-hosted services (Tailscale, WireGuard, etc.)
Evaluate: Railway vs VPN-at-home tradeoffs
Produce recommendation with steps

title: Debug MC subtask PATCH + tighten loose ends
description:
Two parts:

1. BUG: Subtask status updates via PATCH endpoint silently fail — sent subtask status changes, got ok:true back, but data didnt stick. Need to debug the PATCH /{id}/subtasks endpoint and verify the right payload format.

2. CLEANUP: General code tightening — clean up loose ends, remove dead code, ensure consistency across the Mission Control server.

Verify every write by reading back the data after.
details:
Two parts:

1. BUG: Subtask status updates via PATCH endpoint silently fail — sent subtask status changes, got ok:true back, but data didnt stick. Need to debug the PATCH /{id}/subtasks endpoint and verify the right payload format.

2. CLEANUP: General code tightening — clean up loose ends, remove dead code, ensure consistency across the Mission Control server.

Verify every write by reading back the data after.

title: Clean up MC repo — remove stale files, update docs, push to GitHub
